questions.question: Remorse
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Remorse = पश्चाताप / गहरा अफसोस
  • Opposite Idea: Satisfaction = संतोष
  • Example: He felt satisfaction instead of remorse.
  • Exam Tip: Guilt ↔ Contentment pairs are common.

questions.question: Thwart
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Thwart = रोकना / विफल करना
  • Opposite Idea: Allow = अनुमति देना
  • Example: The plan was allowed, not thwarted.
  • Exam Tip: Block ↔ Permit contrasts.

questions.question: Indigenous
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Indigenous = देशज / स्थानीय
  • Opposite Idea: Alien = विदेशी
  • Example: Alien species replaced indigenous ones.
  • Exam Tip: Native ↔ Foreign is frequent.

questions.question: EMBARK
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Embark = शुरू करना
  • Opposite Idea: Finish = समाप्त करना
  • Example: He finished the task he had embarked on.
  • Exam Tip: Begin ↔ End pairs are very common.

questions.question: SUPPRESS
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Suppress = दबाना
  • Opposite Idea: Release = मुक्त करना
  • Example: Emotions should not be suppressed but released.
  • Exam Tip: Control ↔ Free contrasts.

questions.question: SCATTER
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Scatter = बिखेरना
  • Opposite Idea: Gather = इकट्ठा करना
  • Example: Children gathered the books they had scattered.
  • Exam Tip: Collect ↔ Disperse.

questions.question: IMPECCABLE
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Impeccable = निर्दोष
  • Opposite Idea: Blemished = दोषयुक्त
  • Example: The record was blemished, not impeccable.
  • Exam Tip: Perfect ↔ Faulty.

questions.question: CONGENIAL
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Congenial = मिलनसार
  • Opposite Idea: Mean = कठोर / दुष्ट
  • Example: A mean attitude is not congenial.
  • Exam Tip: Friendly ↔ Cruel traits.

questions.question: indolent
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Indolent = आलसी
  • Opposite Idea: Energetic = ऊर्जावान
  • Example: An energetic worker is not indolent.
  • Exam Tip: Lazy ↔ Active is very common.
